# git4aiagents

> Git was designed for humans. Agents need something fundamentally different.

git4aiagents is an open manifesto and design spec for agent-native version control. Git's merge model is semantically blind (it checks lines, not meaning). At human speed that was tolerable. At agent speed (250,000+ agents on a single monorepo), it collapses. Branches become a quarter million divergent realities. PR review becomes an impossible bottleneck. CI never finishes running.

The manifesto answers 14 foundational design questions:

1. Unit of work: semantic mutations + task DAGs (not commits)
2. Coordination: Serializable Snapshot Isolation over a semantic dependency graph
3. Consistency: strong within dependency clusters, eventual across independent ones
4. Reasoning traces: 3-tier (structured metadata, rationale, full LLM dump)
5. Human interaction: policy-based governance, not line-by-line code review
6. Migration: Git bridge for incremental adoption
7. Failure recovery: dependency-aware targeted rollback
8. Identity and trust: cryptographic agent identity + capability scoping + trust scoring
9. Semantic parsing: tree-sitter for block-level parsing across 100+ languages
10. Dependency tracking: multi-signal graph (imports, refs, agent behavior, test coverage)
11. Task decomposition: planner agent + scheduler + task DAG
12. Observability: event streaming to 3 surfaces (ops, stakeholders, audit)
13. Testing: 3-tier (syntactic per-mutation, targeted per-task, integration periodic)
14. Versioning: named snapshots with semantic guarantees

Full content: https://www.git4aiagents.com/llms-full.txt
Website: https://www.git4aiagents.com/
GitHub: https://github.com/lcbasu/git4aiagents
Author: Lokesh Basu (@lcbasu)
